summary: Large lbaudio_ax decomp/matching PR focused on Melee's AX audio library: filled many lbAudioAx stubs, recovered more audio object/userdata and pool state, fixed public prototypes, and renamed an AX aux-heap helper. decomp.dev reported +1780 matched code bytes, +40 matched data bytes, and 6 new matches, but also noted regressions in .bss, fn_80023254, .sdata, fn_800268B4, and the renamed AXDriver_8038E034 item.

key_files.notes: Main implementation work: 1321 added lines replacing many /// stubs and reshaping existing functions for match.
Recovered lbAudioAx_UserData fields and expanded lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc from a small allocator wrapper to a 0x1F554-sized state/heap struct with bank arrays and aux buffers.
Updated signatures from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect types to concrete s32/void parameterized prototypes.

what_changed: Replaced stubs or large unmatched regions in lbaudio_ax.c with C for lbAudioAx_800233EC, lbAudioAx_80023B24, lbAudioAx_800243F4, fn_800253D8, fn_800256BC, fn_80025FAC, fn_800262A0, fn_80026650, fn_800269AC, fn_80026C04, lbAudioAx_80027168, fn_80027488, lbAudioAx_80027648, lbAudioAx_8002785C, lbAudioAx_80027AB0, lbAudioAx_80027DF8, lbAudioAx_8002838C, and lbAudioAx_80028690.
decomp.dev reported new 100% matches for fn_800262A0, lbAudioAx_800243F4, fn_800251EC, fn_80025B44, fn_80025CBC, and lbaudio_ax .sdata2.
Added concrete audio bank/load state behavior: SFX key-off, bank unload/load, pending-load cancellation/waiting, language-dependent audio paths, and stage/player-driven SFX bank selection.

Recovered more of the sound object lifecycle: SoundParams setup, lbAudioAx_UserData initialization, per-frame callback dispatch through ud->x10, pan/volume clamping, and AXDriver pan/volume update calls.
Recovered or reshaped spatial audio pan logic around HSD_GObj user data, entity->classifier, ftLib_80086644 for fighters, and it_8026B294 for items.

Expanded lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc to contain xB4/x194/x274/x354/x434 arrays, AR buffer x514, reverb heap x554, and delay heap xD954; added lbl_804D38D0, lbl_804D3870, lbl_804D3878, and sfx_remap_803BC6A4 data.

Changed public prototypes such as lbAudioAx_80023B24 from f32 to s32, lbAudioAx_800243F4 from UNK_RET to s32, fn_80026650/fn_80027488/lbAudioAx_8002785C/lbAudioAx_80028690 to s32(void), and fn_800269AC/lbAudioAx_80027DF8 to void(void).
Renamed AXDriver_8038E034 to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize and used that semantic name in lbaudio_ax aux heap assertions.

Adjusted data layout, including shrinking data_pad_0 to 0x144 and moving formerly separate lbl_804337C4/lbl_804338A4/lbl_80433984/lbl_80433A64/lbl_80433B44-style arrays into or alongside the recovered pool allocation state.
smart_moves: Recovered structs before chasing every function: replacing raw lbl_80433710 pointer arithmetic with lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc fields made later bank-state and heap code much more searchable and reusable.

Kept uncertain semantics conservative: address-based lbAudioAx_800xxxxx/fn_800xxxxx names remained where no better name was evidenced, while AXDriver_8038E034 was renamed only where the use as an aux heap-size query was clear.
Used path-prefix length lbl_804D38D0 to handle both /audio/ and /audio/us/ string construction without duplicating load logic.
Centralized sound-object initialization through SoundParams and lbAudioAx_UserData fields, then updated pan/volume every frame with explicit 0..0x7F clamps before AXDriver_8038D2B4/AXDriver_8038D3B8.
Preserved compiler-sensitive control flow for matching, such as fallthrough switch increments in lbAudioAx_800243F4 and expression/order tweaks in fn_800251EC instead of over-cleaning the code.
decomp_lessons: Large BSS struct recovery can improve source clarity while temporarily hurting automated data-match reporting; this PR's bot report shows .bss dropping by 56951 bytes after lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc expansion.

When a function's role becomes clear from call sites, a semantic rename can be worthwhile even if the match bot tracks the old address-based item as a regression; here AXDriver_8038E034 became HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize.
Public headers should be updated as soon as signatures are known; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and wrong return types helps downstream functions compile and decompile against the right ABI.
Audio pan/volume code repeatedly clamps logical 0..0x7F values and sends even-scaled values via (value * 2) & 0xFE to AXDriver; future lbaudio work should reuse that idiom.
For HSD_GObj audio callbacks, prefer recovered struct fields like user_data, entity, classifier, current_frame, end_frame, pan_left, and pan_right over raw casts when evidence supports them.
Comments like TODO: This is 100% permute-able are useful breadcrumbs: they mark regions where many statement permutations may affect scheduling without changing semantics.
naming_conventions: Retain lbAudioAx_800xxxxx and fn_800xxxxx names for functions whose semantic names are not yet proven.
Use semantic HSD_* naming for baselib audio helpers when the behavior is evidenced; AXDriver_8038E034 was renamed to HSD_AudioGetAuxHeapSize.
Keep address-commented prototypes in lbaudio_ax.h, e.g. /* 023B24 */ and /* 028690 */, while replacing unknown types with concrete signatures.
Use offset-style field names such as xB4, x194, x274, x354, x434, x514, x554, and xD954 inside recovered structs when field purpose is still uncertain.
Static/global labels stayed in lbl_80xxxxxx form unless the PR had enough evidence for a better name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: lbAudioAx_800243F4 uses a long fallthrough switch that increments var_r6 for specific SFX IDs, preserving case order and fallthrough comments to match generated control flow.
fn_800251EC was tuned with local variable/pad ordering, entity->classifier access, reordered zero assignments, split float multiplication, and ternary absolute value to move from 97.02% to 100%.
fn_80025B44 and fn_80025CBC include tiny no-op-looking blocks `if (ud && ud) {}` and changed comparisons from `<` to `<=`, matching evidence that codegen shape mattered.
Array initialization and shifting use fixed-count loops and unrolled chunks over 0x38-slot state arrays, reflecting compiler-friendly matching rather than idiomatic container code.
Some data remains accessed through char-pointer offsets from lbl_803BB300/lbl_803BB340 where the underlying archive/table structs are not yet fully recovered.
The lbaudio data section was aligned by explicit padding/data declarations such as data_pad_0[0x144] and sfx_remap_803BC6A4[0x4A][2].
Callback signatures were made concrete enough for calls like HSD_SynthSFXLoad(..., fn_80026C04, ...) while still using casts where the original ABI/codegen needed them.
review_feedback: No human review comments or GitHub reviews were present in the slice; the PR body only said: Tracking PR as usual.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code 62.51% (+0.05%, +1780 bytes) and matched data 38.05% (+40 bytes).
Automated report listed 6 new matches: fn_800262A0, lbAudioAx_800243F4, fn_800251EC, fn_80025B44, fn_80025CBC, and lbaudio_ax .sdata2.

Automated report listed 16 improved unmatched lbaudio_ax items, led by lbAudioAx_80027DF8 (+1300 bytes to 94.22%), lbAudioAx_80028690 (+1122 bytes to 95.13%), lbAudioAx_80027AB0, lbAudioAx_8002838C, and lbAudioAx_80023B24.
Automated report also flagged regressions: lbaudio_ax .bss, sysdolphin/baselib/axdriver AXDriver_8038E034, fn_80023254, lbaudio_ax .sdata, and fn_800268B4.
follow_up_queries: lbAudioAx_800243F4 fallthrough switch var_r6 SFX IDs
